verses.latin_vulgate: Quo abiit dilectus tuus, o pulcherrima mulierum ?  quo declinavit dilectus tuus ?  et quæremus eum tecum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֚נָה הָלַ֣ךְ דּוֹדֵ֔ךְ הַיָּפָ֖ה בַּנָּשִׁ֑ים אָ֚נָה פָּנָ֣ה דוֹדֵ֔ךְ וּנְבַקְשֶׁ֖נּוּ עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whither is thy beloved gone, O thou most beautiful among women? whither is thy beloved turned aside, and we will seek him with thee?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuhunka ystäväs on mennyt, o sinä kaikkein kaunein vaimoin seassa! kuhunka ystäväs on kääntänyt itsensä? Me etsimme häntä sinun kanssas.
verses.king_james: Whither is thy beloved gone, O thou fairest among women? whither is thy beloved turned aside? that we may seek him with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dilectus meus descendit in hortum suum ad areolam aromatum,  ut pascatur in hortis, et lilia colligat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דּוֹדִי֙ יָרַ֣ד לְגַנּ֔וֹ לַעֲרֻג֖וֹת הַבֹּ֑שֶׂם לִרְעוֹת֙ בַּגַּנִּ֔ים וְלִלְקֹ֖ט שֽׁוֹשַׁנִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: My beloved is gone down into his garden, to the bed of aromatical spices, to feed in the gardens, and to gather lilies.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun ystäväni on mennyt kryytimaahansa, yrttisarkoihinsa, että hän siellä kryytimaassa kaitsis laumaansa, ja hakis ruusuja.
verses.king_james: My beloved is gone down into his garden, to the beds of spices, to feed in the gardens, and to gather lilies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego dilecto meo, et dilectus meus mihi,  qui pascitur inter lilia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲנִ֤י לְדוֹדִי֙ וְדוֹדִ֣י לִ֔י הָרֹעֶ֖ה בַּשּׁוֹשַׁנִּֽים׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: I to my beloved, and my beloved to me, who feedeth among the lilies.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun ystäväni on minun ja minä olen hänen, joka kaitsee kukkasten keskellä.
verses.king_james: I am my beloved’s, and my beloved is mine: he feedeth among the lilies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pulchra es, amica mea ;  suavis, et decora sicut Jerusalem ;  terribilis ut castrorum acies ordinata.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָפָ֨ה אַ֤תְּ רַעְיָתִי֙ כְּתִרְצָ֔ה נָאוָ֖ה כִּירוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם אֲיֻמָּ֖ה כַּנִּדְגָּלֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou art beautiful, O my love, sweet and comely as Jerusalem terrible as an army set in array.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä olet ihana, minun kultani, niinkuin Tirtsa, kaunis niinkuin Jerusalem, peljättävä niinkuin sotajoukko.
verses.king_james: Thou art beautiful, O my love, as Tirzah, comely as Jerusalem, terrible as an army with banners.

verses.latin_vulgate: Averte oculos tuos a me,  quia ipsi me avolare fecerunt.  Capilli tui sicut grex caprarum  quæ apparuerunt de Galaad.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָסֵ֤בִּי עֵינַ֙יִךְ֙ מִנֶּגְדִּ֔י שֶׁ֥הֵ֖ם הִרְהִיבֻ֑נִי שַׂעְרֵךְ֙ כְּעֵ֣דֶר הָֽעִזִּ֔ים שֶׁגָּלְשׁ֖וּ מִן־הַגִּלְעָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Turn away thy eyes from me, for they have made me flee away. Thy hair is as a flock of goats, that appear from Galaad.
verses.finnish_biblia: Käännä silmäs pois minusta; sillä ne minun sytyttävät; sinun hiukses ovat niinkuin vuohilauma, jotka Gileadin vuorella kerityt ovat.
verses.king_james: Turn away thine eyes from me, for they have overcome me: thy hair is as a flock of goats that appear from Gilead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dentes tui sicut grex ovium  quæ ascenderunt de lavacro :  omnes gemellis fœtibus,  et sterilis non est in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁנַּ֙יִךְ֙ כְּעֵ֣דֶר הָֽרְחֵלִ֔ים שֶׁעָל֖וּ מִן־הָרַחְצָ֑ה שֶׁכֻּלָּם֙ מַתְאִימ֔וֹת וְשַׁכֻּלָ֖ה אֵ֥ין בָּהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thy teeth as a flock of sheep, which come up from the washing, all with twins, and there is none barren among them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinun hampas ovat niinkuin lammaslauma, jotka pesosta nousevat, ja kaikki kaksoisia kantavat, ja ei yksikään ole heistä hedelmätöin.
verses.king_james: Thy teeth are as a flock of sheep which go up from the washing, whereof every one beareth twins, and there is not one barren among them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut cortex mali punici, sic genæ tuæ,  absque occultis tuis.
verses.douay_rheims: Thy cheeks are as the bark of a pomegranate, beside what is hidden within thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinun kasvos ovat niinkuin granatomenan lohkot sinun palmikkois välillä.
verses.king_james: As a piece of a pomegranate are thy temples within thy locks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sexaginta sunt reginæ, et octoginta concubinæ,  et adolescentularum non est numerus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁשִּׁ֥ים הֵ֙מָּה֙ מְלָכ֔וֹת וּשְׁמֹנִ֖ים פִּֽילַגְשִׁ֑ים וַעֲלָמ֖וֹת אֵ֥ין מִסְפָּֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: There are threescore queens, and fourscore concubines, and young maidens without number.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuusikymmentä on drottninkia ja kahdeksankymmentä vaimoa, ja neitseet ovat epälukuiset;
verses.king_james: There are threescore queens, and fourscore concubines, and virgins without number.

verses.latin_vulgate: Una est columba mea, perfecta mea,  una est matris suæ, electa genetrici suæ.  Viderunt eam filiæ, et beatissimam prædicaverunt ;  reginæ et concubinæ, et laudaverunt eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַחַ֥ת הִיא֙ יוֹנָתִ֣י תַמָּתִ֔י אַחַ֥ת הִיא֙ לְאִמָּ֔הּ בָּרָ֥ה הִ֖יא לְיֽוֹלַדְתָּ֑הּ רָא֤וּהָ בָנוֹת֙ וַֽיְאַשְּׁר֔וּהָ מְלָכ֥וֹת וּפִֽילַגְשִׁ֖ים וַֽיְהַלְלֽוּהָ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: One is my dove, my perfect one is but one, she is the only one of her mother, the chosen of her that bore her. The daughters saw her, and declared her most blessed: the queens and concubines, and they praised her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta yksi on minun kyhkyläiseni ja minun täydelliseni; yksi on äitinsä rakkain ja synnyttäjänsä valittu: koska tyttäret hänen näkivät, ylistivät he hänen autuaaksi, kuningattaret ja vaimot ylistivät häntä.
verses.king_james: My dove, my undefiled is but one; she is the only one of her mother, she is the choice one of her that bare her. The daughters saw her, and blessed her; yea, the queens and the concubines, and they praised her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ est ista quæ progreditur quasi aurora consurgens,  pulchra ut luna, electa ut sol,  terribilis ut castrorum acies ordinata ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִי־זֹ֥את הַנִּשְׁקָפָ֖ה כְּמוֹ־שָׁ֑חַר יָפָ֣ה כַלְּבָנָ֗ה בָּרָה֙ כַּֽחַמָּ֔ה אֲיֻמָּ֖ה כַּנִּדְגָּלֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Who is she that cometh forth as the morning rising, fair as the moon, bright as the sun, terrible as an army set in array?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka on tämä, joka nähdään niinkuin aamurusko, ihana niinkuin kuu, valittu niinkuin aurinko, peljättävä niinkuin sotajoukko?
verses.king_james: Who is she that looketh forth as the morning, fair as the moon, clear as the sun, and terrible as an army with banners?

verses.latin_vulgate: Descendi in hortum nucum,  ut viderem poma convallium,  et inspicerem si floruisset vinea,  et germinassent mala punica.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶל־גִּנַּ֤ת אֱגוֹז֙ יָרַ֔דְתִּי לִרְא֖וֹת בְּאִבֵּ֣י הַנָּ֑חַל לִרְאוֹת֙ הֲפָֽרְחָ֣ה הַגֶּ֔פֶן הֵנֵ֖צוּ הָרִמֹּנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: I went down into the garden of nuts, to see the fruits of the valleys, and to look if the vineyard had flourished, and the pomegranates budded.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen mennyt alas yrttitarhaani, katsomaan vesoja ojan reunalla, kurkistelemaan, jos viinapuut kukoistivat, jos granatomenat viheriöitsivät.
verses.king_james: I went down into the garden of nuts to see the fruits of the valley, and to see whether the vine flourished, and the pomegranates budded.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nescivi : anima mea conturbavit me,  propter quadrigas Aminadab.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֣א יָדַ֔עְתִּי נַפְשִׁ֣י שָׂמַ֔תְנִי מַרְכְּב֖וֹת עַמִּ֥י נָדִֽיב׃
verses.douay_rheims: I knew not: my soul troubled me for the chariots of Aminadab.
verses.finnish_biblia: En minä tiennyt, että minun sieluni minun hamaan minun mieluisen kansani vaunuihin asti asettanut oli.
verses.king_james: Or ever I was aware, my soul made me like the chariots of Amminadib.

verses.latin_vulgate: Revertere, revertere, Sulamitis !  revertere, revertere ut intueamur te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׁ֤וּבִי שׁ֙וּבִי֙ הַשּׁ֣וּלַמִּ֔ית שׁ֥וּבִי שׁ֖וּבִי וְנֶחֱזֶה־בָּ֑ךְ מַֽה־תֶּחֱזוּ֙ בַּשּׁ֣וּלַמִּ֔ית כִּמְחֹלַ֖ת הַֽמַּחֲנָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Return, return, O Sulamitess: return, return that we may behold thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Palaja, palaja Sulamit: palaja, palaja nähdäksemme sinua. Mitä te näitte Sulamitissa, paitsi sotajoukkoin tanssia?
verses.king_james: Return, return, O Shulamite; return, return, that we may look upon thee. What will ye see in the Shulamite? As it were the company of two armies.
